items.passage: Soil is a complex mixture of broken rock, decayed plant matter, water, and tiny living organisms. The top layer, called topsoil, is usually the most fertile because it contains the most organic material from fallen leaves. Beneath it lies subsoil, which is denser and richer in clay particles than the layer above it. Healthy soil takes hundreds of years to form, but it can be lost in a single season through erosion.

items.passage: Gravitational waves are ripples in space and time caused by the violent movement of very massive objects. The discovery allowed astronomers to study events that no ordinary telescope can ever observe. New detectors are now being built around the world to listen for even fainter signals from deeper space.
